challenges.title: Custom Tooltip with ::before
challenges.description: Create a CSS-only tooltip that appears on hover using ::before pseudo-element.
requirements: Button or span that acts as tooltip trigger
Tooltip appears above the element on hover
Tooltip has a background, text color, and border radius
Tooltip has a small arrow at the bottom (CSS triangle)
No JavaScript allowed

solution.html: Hover over me
solution.explanation: Uses ::before for tooltip text content and ::after for the arrow (CSS triangle). Both are hidden by default and shown on :hover. The data-tooltip attribute provides the tooltip text.

challenges.title: Styled Blockquote with Decorative Quotes
challenges.description: Create a styled blockquote with large decorative quotation marks using ::before and ::after.
requirements: Blockquote with background and left border
Large opening quote before the text
Large closing quote after the text
Citation text below the quote
Quotes should be positioned absolutely

solution.html:
CSS pseudo-elements are incredibly powerful for adding decorative content without cluttering your HTML.

- A CSS Developer
solution.explanation: ::before adds an opening quote character, ::after adds a closing quote. Both are absolutely positioned within the relative blockquote.

challenges.title: Article with Drop Cap and Styled First Line
challenges.description: Create an article page that uses ::first-letter for a drop cap and ::first-line for the first line styling.
requirements: Article with title and multiple paragraphs
First paragraph has a drop cap using ::first-letter
First paragraph's first line has different styling (::first-line)
Drop cap should be 3x the normal font size
Drop cap should have a different color

solution.html:
The Art of Typography

Typography is the art and technique of arranging type to make written language legible, readable, and visually appealing. The first letter of this paragraph is styled as a drop cap, a classic typographic technique that draws the reader's eye to the beginning of the content.

The Details Matter

This second paragraph doesn't have the special styling. Only the first paragraph gets the drop cap treatment and the styled first line, creating a visually interesting entry point into the article.
solution.explanation: p:first-of-type::first-letter creates the drop cap on the first paragraph only. p:first-of-type::first-line styles the first line of the first paragraph. Both use the :first-of-type pseudo-class combined with pseudo-elements.

challenges.title: CSS-Only Star Rating with ::before
challenges.description: Create a star rating component using only CSS pseudo-elements and checkboxes. Stars should be filled based on hover/checked state.
requirements: 5 stars displayed inline
Stars change color on hover (filled state)
Checked stars stay filled
Use ::before with content: '★'
No JavaScript, no images

solution.html: Click to rate (CSS only)
solution.explanation: Labels use ::before { content: '★' } to display stars. Flex-direction: row-reverse allows the ~ sibling combinator to work for hover/checked states going backwards.

challenges.title: Full Pseudo-element UI Components
challenges.description: Build multiple UI components that rely entirely on pseudo-elements for their visual styling. Include a progress bar, notification badge, custom checkbox, image overlay, and breadcrumb navigation.
requirements: Progress bar: filled state using ::before with width percentage
Notification badge: ::after on bell icon showing count
Custom checkbox: ::before for checkmark, ::after for box
Image overlay: ::before on image container for hover effect
Breadcrumb: ::after { content: '>' } between items (not on last)

solution.html:
Progress Bar

Notification Badge

🔔

Breadcrumb

Home Products Category Current Page

Image Overlay

Image Placeholder
solution.explanation: Progress bar uses ::before for fill. Notification bell uses ::after for badge (content from data-count attribute). Breadcrumb uses ::after on all but last link. Image overlay uses ::before with hover transition.
